name: Loomly Product Overview
heading: __MISSING__
product_overview_description:
Loomly is a social media marketing tool that simplifies content creation, scheduling, and analytics. Its calendar view helps plan posts across multiple platforms. Teams can collaborate easily, ensuring consistent and high-quality social media campaigns. Loomly offers insights and analytics to track performance and build better social media strategies. Its automated publishing and post ideas feature helps maintain a consistent posting schedule and generate fresh content ideas. For agencies, Loomly streamlines social media management, improves client collaboration, and provides detailed reports, making it an ideal tool for managing multiple client accounts efficiently.

Source: G2

Features

  • Helps with post-planning and scheduling
  • Offers to create a dedicated content calendar for different brands 
  • Comes with automated publishing and near-native publishing options
  • Gives additional tips to optimize your content for each social channel 
  • Real-time collaboration and communication tools across teams 
  • Export your content calendar for offline collaboration
  • Has advanced analytical options for tracking parameters 
  • Helps categorize posts under different labels 
  • Respond to comments, mentions, and messages across all connected channels
  • Get inspirations from Loomly Post Ideas and X trends
  • Use Hashtag Manager for relevant suggestions and trending hashtags

Pros 

✅Getting started with the platform is pretty easy 

✅Offers an intuitive social media calendar view 

✅Users say that they find it quite affordable 

Cons 

❌Users say that the analytics and metrics can be much more insightful and useful 

❌No AI capabilities

Pricing Module 

Loomly starts its pricing with $32 Per Month for its base plan and offers a 15-day trial. Offers a standard version of $60 Per Month, including an advanced and a premium range at $131 and $277 respectively, however, in comparison to Synup, people often feel that Loomly doesn’t provide any additional capabilities and costs just as much, which they think is too much for a social media management platform.
